items.title: Families calling out to loved ones trapped in rubble by Venezuela quakes
items.summary: In La Guaira, desperate families keep vigil at buildings where they fear their relatives are trapped, but face an impossible task to move heavy debris.

items.title: In Caracas, this feels like the hardest moment in Venezuela's modern history
items.summary: Rescue teams are working ceaselessly to reach those trapped under rubble. But as hope fades, anger is growing.

items.title: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire
items.summary: Iran says it has launched retaliatory attacks at US infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain after the US said it hit multiple targets across Iran.

items.title: Why Kim Jong Un never talks about his mother - or her controversial bloodline
items.summary: Very few North Koreans know about her, as her origin could threaten the regime’s legitimacy.

items.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal
items.summary: Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.

items.title: Heatwave breaks records in Germany, Denmark and Czech Republic
items.summary: An estimated 150 million people have been experiencing temperatures of over 35C across Europe.

items.title: Trump's face is added to select US passports for America's 250th birthday
items.summary: The commemorative special edition passports will be available on 6 July, and can only be applied for in person

items.title: 117 dead dogs found at California 'no-kill' animal rescue - many with gunshot wounds
items.summary: Hundreds more animals are still missing from the California facility as investigators continue excavating the grounds.

items.title: Australia to double maximum penalty for platforms in breach of social media ban
items.summary: Children under the age of 16 have been prevented from 10 key social media platforms in Australia since December 2025.

items.title: Budapest's first Pride since Orban left power
items.summary: Budapest held its first Pride march since the end of former prime minister Viktor Orban's 16-year government with thousands turning out to celebrate.

items.title: Burkina Faso severs diplomatic ties with France
items.summary: The West African country's junta accused the former colonial power of undermining its national interests.

items.title: Uganda's leading media outlets say they are under 'military siege'
items.summary: The army chief says he does not believe in a free press and has ordered the shutdown of NTV and Daily Monitor.

items.title: Religion row as Texas makes Bible stories required reading in schools
items.summary: Critics say the new reading requirements infringe on religious freedoms and blur the separation of church and state.

items.title: Meloni and Trump: A very public fall-out that is proving very hard to fix
items.summary: Italy's PM was not long ago being called the "Trump whisperer", but their relationship has gone from public attacks to personal insults.

items.title: Billionaire Leon Black walks out of Epstein investigation hearing
items.summary: The House panel then issued subpoenas demanding the private equity tycoon, who co-founded Apollo Global Management, testify on camera and under oath.

items.title: Pete Buttigieg briefly separated from children after false police report
items.summary: Buttigieg says the day he spent separated from his twins was "among the darkest hours of my life".

items.title: Ex-Trump adviser John Bolton pleads guilty to mishandling classified documents
items.summary: Bolton faces a prison sentence of up to five years and has agreed to pay $2.25m in fine, prosecutors say.

items.title: DR Congo takes Rwanda to international court over decades of conflict
items.summary: Kinshasa accuses its neighbour of committing various violations since the 1994 Rwanda genocide.

items.title: Mother dies saving daughter in Venezuela earthquakes
items.summary: Héctor Bello wrote on social media that "you gave your own life for our daughter" in the quakes which killed at least 920 people.

items.title: Bad Bunny lights up London with history-making stadium show
items.summary: Pride and a party as the Puerto Rican becomes the first Latin artist to headline a UK stadium.

items.title: What news reports from 1600s tell us about life in Mughal India
items.summary: Thousands of forgotten news reports are reshaping our understanding of Aurangzeb and the Mughal world.

items.title: I'm in therapy for my 14-hour-a-day phone addiction and I'm determined to beat it
items.summary: Addiction treatment centres say more clients are coming for help with "out-of-control" phone use.

items.title: Sort Your Life Out: The four most common cluttering mistakes and how to fix them
items.summary: Tidying expert Dilly Carter shares her best tips on organising your kitchen, lounge and clothes.

items.title: Millie Bobby Brown and Louis Partridge on belly laughs, sibling vibes and Enola Holmes
items.summary: Brown talks to the BBC about having her "hands and heart" all over the detective adventure series.

items.title: These women said no to having kids - here's why
items.summary: From financial constraints to worries about the environment, women tell the BBC why they do not want kids.

items.title: Panic attacks and fractures - Venezuela hospital treats earthquake victims
items.summary: The BBC travelled to a Caracas hospital that is treating people from the worst affected areas.

items.title: Watch: A race against the clock for Venezuela earthquake rescuers
items.summary: There is a sense that the next few hours will be critical for saving anyone who is still trapped, says the BBC's Dan Johnson reporting from the border of Venezuela and Columbia.

items.title: Newborn baby rescued from Venezuela earthquake rubble
items.summary: Two powerful earthquakes rocked Venezuela within seconds of each other on Wednesday, killing at least 920 people.

items.title: Watch: BBC reports from La Guaira, one of Venezuela's worst-hit areas
items.summary: Vanessa Silva visited the coastal state, where rescuers are still working to find survivors under the rubble after twin earthquakes rocked the country.
